messages.content:
Statistics: What are the defining characteristics of the Gaussian distribution? *
Height and width
Height and position
Position and width
All of the above
None of the above
I don’t know
All of the above

Calculus: What is the solution to this equation: dx/dt = -a*x +b *
x(t) = -a*t + b + x0
x(t) = x0*log(-a*t) + b/a
x(t) = x0*exp(-a*t) + b/a
x(t) = x0*exp(-a*t) 
x(t) = x0*sin(-a*t + b)
I don’t know
x(t) = -a*t + b + x0

Linear Algebra: What is the importance of eigenvalues and eigenvectors of a matrix? *
They tell us something about the matrix transformation.
They provide an equivalent system of a matrix.
They provide a geometric explanation of the matrix properties.
All of the above.
None of the above.
I don’t know.
All of the above.

Model fitting: What's the least-biased method of evaluating the performance of a data-generated model? *
Cross-validation
Mean squared error of fit
Significance testing of fit parameters
All of the above
None of the above
I don’t know
Cross-validation

GLM: Generalized linear models allow you to: *
Get causal insight into brain function
Generalize your model results to other areas
Fit responses that are not normally distributed
All of the above
None of the above
I don’t know
Fit responses that are not normally distributed.

Dimensionality reduction: Dimensionality reduction is *
A supervised learning technique
A way to extract structure from data
A method to remove nonlinearities in the data
All of the above
None of the above
I don’t know
A way to extract structure from data.

Reinforcement learning: In updating the value function (V := V + a*d), if "d" equals the error, then the parameter “a” represents the: *
Reward Prediction Error
Expected Future Reward
Discount Factor
Learning Rate
I don’t know
Learning Rate

Deep Learning: In an auto-encoder architecture, the relationship between the number of inputs and outputs is: *
# inputs greater than # outputs
# inputs less than  # outputs
# inputs equals # outputs
I don’t know
# inputs equals # outputs

Bayes: Bayes Rule states that P(y|x) = P(x|y)*P(y), the term P(x|y) in this case is often called: *
Posterior
Prior
Likelihood
Bayes Factor
I don’t know
Likelihood

Real Neuron Models: In a Leaky Integrate and Fire model, a high membrane time constant would indicate that for a constant input current the neuron has: *
High spike rate
Low spike rate
High membrane resistance
Low resting membrane potential
I don’t know
Low spike rate
